hero.subheadline: Obsidia builds custom automations, websites, and applications for businesses that are serious about how they operate.

problem.label: Findings
problem.d01statement: The operation has capacity gaps, not insight gaps.
problem.d01note: You know what is broken. You need someone to build the fix.
problem.d02statement: Most proposals describe a goal, not a schedule.
problem.d02note: Phases and milestones are not a plan. A build calendar is.
problem.d03statement: The tool outlasting the engagement is not optional.
problem.d03note: Obsidia builds for hand-off. Not for tenure.

homeServices.automationDesc: Replace manual processes with digital workflows that run themselves. Approvals, data sync, reporting.
homeServices.websitesDesc: Sites built to convert visitors and load fast. Engineered for performance.
homeServices.appsDesc: Custom tools for exactly how your team operates. Internal portals, dashboards, integrations.

stats.claim0: A full work week reclaimed, every month
stats.detail0: By eliminating manual handoffs, approval loops, and repeated data entry — time compounds back to your team permanently.
stats.claim1: Approval cycles collapse from days to minutes
stats.detail1: A structural fix to how decisions move through the business. Not a shortcut — a redesign of the process itself.
stats.claim2: Less than half the overhead. Same output.
stats.detail2: Savings compound over time. The automation runs at 2am, on weekends, and at scale — without adding headcount.

pillars.label: Obsidia Standards
pillars.subtext:
Applied to every engagement,
without exception.
pillars.p01body: Every engagement starts with a structured process map, not a proposal. We do not design automation for a workflow we have not observed. The brief is never complete until we have seen the work firsthand.
pillars.p02body: First conversation to live workflow in 4 to 8 weeks on most engagements. Urgency is a design constraint, not a risk. We scope for it from day one.
pillars.p03body: Full documentation and a team walkthrough are required deliverables on every project. When the engagement ends, your team runs it — not us.

cta.label: Start a Project
cta.subtext: The first conversation costs nothing. The last deliverable is yours to run.
cta.step01desc: 20 minutes. You leave with a prioritised list of what to fix and why.
cta.step02desc: Full workflow audit. Fixed-fee proposal delivered in 3 business days.
cta.step03desc: 4 to 8 weeks. Documented. Yours to run the moment we leave.

faq.q0: What kinds of workflows can you automate?
faq.a0: Anything that has a clear trigger and a repeatable outcome: approval chains, data syncing between tools, report generation, lead routing, invoice processing, onboarding sequences, and more. If your team does it manually on a schedule or in response to an event, it can almost certainly be automated.
faq.q1: How long does a typical project take?
faq.a1: Most workflows go live within 4–6 weeks of the first conversation. Simple, single-system automations can be done in under two weeks. Multi-system integrations with complex logic take longer. We give you a realistic timeline after the audit, not before.
faq.q2: Do we need any technical knowledge on our end?
faq.a2: No. We handle all the technical work. You need to be able to describe your current process clearly and tell us what a good outcome looks like. We do the architecture, the build, and the documentation. Your team learns to monitor the output, not the code.
faq.q3: Which tools do you work with?
faq.a3: We work with any tool that has an API or native integration support: n8n, Make, Zapier, HubSpot, Notion, Airtable, Slack, Google Workspace, Monday.com, and many others. If the tool exists, there is almost certainly a way to connect it.
faq.q4: What happens if something breaks after launch?
faq.a4: The first 30 days of fixes are included at no charge. We monitor for errors during that period and resolve anything that surfaces. After 30 days, optional maintenance packages are available. Every workflow comes with full documentation so you're never dependent on us.
faq.q5: How is Obsidia different from hiring a developer or using a no-code tool ourselves?
faq.a5: A developer builds to your brief, which means you need to know what to brief. A no-code tool requires your team to learn it, maintain it, and debug it. We do all three: we map the process, design the logic, build the automation, and hand it over with documentation. You get the outcome without the overhead.

footer.tagline: Workflow automations, websites, and applications. Built to spec, handed off complete.

notFound.label: Page not found
notFound.body: The page you are looking for does not exist or has been moved. Use the links below to get back on track.

process.label: How We Work
process.defaultHeadline: From first call to live automation.
process.defaultStep01detail: We spend time with your team mapping every manual touchpoint and ranking each by cost. You get a prioritised list of what to automate before we design anything.
process.defaultStep02detail: We architect the full logic (triggers, conditions, transformations) then share the design for your sign-off. No code is written until you've seen exactly how it works.
process.defaultStep03detail: We build and test in a staging environment that mirrors production. Every workflow ships with full documentation, structured logging, and retry logic.
process.defaultStep04detail: We deploy to production, walk your team through every workflow, and hand over complete documentation. The first 30 days of fixes are included.

approach.heroBody: Four phases. No exceptions. Every project runs the same process from first audit to final handoff.
approach.principlesHeadline: The standards we hold ourselves to.
approach.phase01descriptor: Map every manual step before we design anything.
approach.phase01body: We document every workflow before anything is designed — every manual step ranked by time cost before a single line of logic is written.
approach.phase02descriptor: Full logic blueprint, reviewed and signed off by you.
approach.phase02body: Every decision point and branch condition mapped and reviewed with you line by line before build begins.
approach.phase03descriptor: Staged build, tested against real data and edge cases.
approach.phase03body: Staged against real data samples — every edge case from the design phase tested explicitly before production.
approach.phase04descriptor: Deployed with your team present. 30 days included.
approach.phase04body: Deployed with your team present. Full documentation, admin access, and 30 days of support included.
approach.p01body: Every project starts with a process map. We never design automation for a workflow we have not observed ourselves. The brief is never complete until we have seen the work.
approach.p02body: You should be able to run what we build without us present. Full documentation and a team walkthrough are required deliverables on every engagement, without exception.
approach.p03body: Every automation has a defined success metric agreed before build begins. If we cannot measure it, we have not scoped it correctly.
approach.ctaBody: Tell us what you're working on. We'll tell you if it makes sense to build it.

contact.heroTagline: We respond within one hour during business hours.
contact.descriptionPlaceholder: What's slowing you down? What does success look like?
contact.submittedBody: We'll be in touch within one hour.
contact.errEmailInvalid: Please enter a valid email address

services.heroTagline: Automation, websites, and applications — three disciplines delivered by one team that understands how they connect.
services.automationTagline: Replace manual processes with self-executing digital workflows.
services.automationSummary: We replace the manual, repetitive processes your team runs every day with digital workflows that execute themselves — approval chains, data syncs, reporting pipelines, and custom operations built to your exact specification.
services.automationOutcome0: Approval cycles cut from days to under four minutes
services.automationOutcome1: Cross-system data moves without manual entry or errors
services.automationOutcome2: Recurring reports that build, format, and deliver themselves on schedule
services.websitesTagline: Sites built to convert, not just to exist.
services.websitesSummary: We build websites that do real work: landing pages that convert, corporate sites that establish credibility, and web applications that users actually come back to. Fast, precise, and built to last.
services.websitesOutcome0: Purpose-built for your audience and conversion goals
services.websitesOutcome1: Sub-2s load time as a baseline requirement
services.websitesOutcome2: Full documentation, CMS access, and team training on delivery
services.appsTagline: Software built for how your team actually operates.
services.appsSummary: We build the software your team actually needs — mobile apps, internal tools, client portals, and dashboards designed around how your business works, not how off-the-shelf software wants it to work.
services.appsOutcome0: Built to your workflow, not adapted from a generic template
services.appsOutcome1: Mobile and desktop — native quality or progressive web app
services.appsOutcome2: First 30 days of fixes and adjustments included on every engagement
services.phase01desc: Every workflow documented before anything is designed.
services.phase02desc: Full logic blueprint reviewed and signed off by you.
services.phase03desc: Staged against real data, tested against every edge case.
services.phase04desc: Deployed live with your team present. 30 days included.
